design_philosophy.core_loop: Qualify within time limit → race against 19 AI opponents while maintaining position threshold → complete laps to progress → unlock wet weather challenges and team upgrades
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Constant position maintenance pressure: players must stay above a countdown position limit or face game over, creating escalating difficulty as races progress
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Compete in a Formula One championship season, earning team promotions and faster cars through consistent performance across real 1989 F1 circuits
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Strict qualification gatekeeping (45-second timer enforces entry barrier) and position-based failure condition create a high-stakes, racing-sim-forward design that values planning and consistent execution over rubber-banding or assists. The World Championship mode treats racing as career progression tied to performance, not just repeated arcade runs.

difficulty.continues_system: Game Over on qualification timeout or falling below position threshold during race; no traditional lives
difficulty.checkpointing: Qualification lap acts as gating checkpoint; World Championship mode uses password system for save persistence across ~2 hour season
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player selects transmission type (automatic, 4-speed manual, 7-speed manual) before each race, affecting handling complexity and skill expression; qualifying lap performance directly determines grid position and race difficulty

progression.upgrade_system: Invitational team promotions grant faster cars during World Championship season based on performance; transmission selection modulates difficulty race-by-race
progression.unlock_mechanism: Completing races in 3rd place or better unlocks wet weather race variants; team invitations unlock higher-performance vehicles as season progresses
replayability_hooks: Multiple transmission difficulties for skill scaling
Wet weather unlocks provide mechanical variation
World Championship spanning all 1989 F1 circuits (Brands Hatch, Hockenheimring, etc.)
Password system allows multiple save states for season runs
8-player arcade cabinet linkage in original arcade version

world_building_techniques: Real 1989 Formula One championship structure (actual circuits, realistic grid competition)
Team invitation system mirrors real F1 career progression mechanics
Fictional Monaco circuit design incorporates recognizable real-world track features
Rear-view mirror UI element grounds player in driver perspective

faction_indicators: Racing team affiliation system (player earns better team invitations based on season performance)
Grid position hierarchy (top 3 finishes grant privilege and progression)
danger_signals: Position countdown mechanic creates moment-to-moment failure risk
Qualification timeout forces quick decision-making under pressure
AI opponent density (19 drivers) creates crowded, challenging race conditions
environmental_storytelling: Arcade cabinet force-feedback steering wheel provides tactile F1 circuit fidelity
Real circuit names ground player in authentic racing geography
Transmission selection (automatic vs. manual gears) signals difficulty commitment to arcade operators and other players

sources_used: wikipedia - Super Monaco GP (gameplay, progression systems, world championship mode, transmission mechanics, qualifying rules, team invitations, password saves)
